steps: Put a baking paper lined roasting tin in the oven and heat to 200°C.
After heated, add EVOO to the roasting tin and put back into the oven to heat.
Cut potatoes into 5cm pieces and place into a large pan.
Add enough tap water to cover the potatoes, add salt and bring to a boiled.
Once boiling, put on a timer and simmer uncovered for 2 minutes.
Drain potatoes in a colander and shake back and forth to fluff up outsides.
Sprinkle with flour and shake to evenly cover.
Carefully put potatoes into the heated oil in the roasting tin and roll them to cover in oil, and spread into a single layer.
Roast for 15 minutes, then take out and turn them over and place back in the oven.
Roast for 15 more minutes and turn over again.
Roast for a final 10-20 minutes until golden and crisp - scatter with salt and serve immediately!

notes: Apparently, the best potatoes for roasting are Yukon Gold and red potatoes.

recipe_info.title: Roast potatoes
recipe_info.intro: A simple, solid, twice cooked roast potato recipe. It takes some time, so plan ahead! Note that the macros assume you're not drinking all of the EVOO from the pan...
